title: hypericum ciliatum var. angustifolium
intro: Hypericum ciliatum var. angustifolium, also known as narrow-leaved St. John's wort, is a perennial plant species that belongs to the Hypericaceae family. It has been used for centuries in traditional medicine due to its medicinal properties, which include antiseptic, anti-inflammatory, and antimicrobial properties, making it effective in treating wounds, skin irritations, and infections. The health benefits of Hypericum ciliatum var. angustifolium include its ability to reduce anxiety and depression, improve sleep quality, and boost the immune system, while also exhibiting antioxidant and anti-cancer properties. In herbal preparations, the plant's flowers, leaves, and stems can be used to make teas, tinctures, salves, and infusions, which can be used topically or internally to harness its medicinal and health benefits.
